case_title: RICHARD BELTRANE VS. EDMUND T. HUANG ET AL

docket_entries.description: AWARD FILED BY ARBITRATOR

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF RICHARD BELTRAME'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THERE IS A LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF AMENDMENT AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1324. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY DETERMINED THAT DISCOVERY WAS OPEN AND WILL RE-AFFIRM THAT RULING. THE COURT GRANTS RELIEF FROM CCP 1141.24 IF THAT IS DEFENDANT'S BASIS FOR ASSERTING THAT DISCOVERY IS CLOSED. DISCOVERY REMAINS OPEN BASED ON THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE. ANY PARTY MAY APPLY TO DEPARTMENT 206 FOR A TRIAL CONTINUANCE IF THAT IS DEEMED NECESSARY. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Richard Beltrame's Motion To Amend Complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and the motion complies with CRC 3.1324. The court previously determined that discovery was open and will re-affirm that ruling. The court grants relief from CCP 1141.24 if that is defendant's basis for asserting that discovery is closed. Discovery remains open based on the current trial date. Any party may apply to department 206 for a trial continuance if that is deemed necessary. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-23-2015 Set For Trial On Mar-07-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL WHITE, TEL. NO. 1-925-447-6031 APPEZRING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SHELLEY KRAMER, TEL. NO. 1-707-7992 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RICHARD BELTRANE's Motion To Amend Complaint is DENIED for failure to comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1324. A motion to amend a pleading before trial must include a copy of the proposed amendment or amended pleading. (Ibid.) Plaintiff's contention that this rule conflicts with California's liberal policy of allowing amended pleadings is colorable. Defendant fails to demonstrate that his contention that discovery is closed is supported by Code of Civil Procedure section 2024.020(a), which establishes that discovery closes 30 days before the date initially set for trial. Trial begins in March 2016, in 117 days. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
